sensoryProfile.sight: Ward-stones brightening along the narrowing road as golden Eversong gives way to leaner mountain country.
sensoryProfile.sound: Mountain wind in the cut and the ward network tightening into a stronger bass vibration.
sensoryProfile.smell: Stone dust, pine, distant water, and colder air replacing the sweeter warmth of the inner woods.
sensoryProfile.touch: Loose gravel under hoof, sharper pulses from the stones, and the bodily sensation of crossing from certainty into exposure.
sensoryProfile.atmosphere: Threshold country: a defensive bottleneck where the comfort of home thins into border vigilance.

establishedFacts.fact: The pass is the southern choke point into Quel'Thalas, with ward-stones placed more densely as the land narrows.
The transition into the pass is palpable in temperature, terrain, and the way the ward network feels to riders and mounts.
Crossing out of the ward grid strips away the magical dampening buffer and exposes travelers to the full pressure of the open leylines.
The pass is the last point of contained elven order before the road turns into exposed Lordaeron countryside with visible civilian collapse.

eventsHere.event: Sylvanas rides south in hard focus, using the road to think through what kind of threat the detained mage might represent.
Jaina's escort rides out of the pass, the ward-hum falls away, and the contamination hits at full strength once they leave the elven buffer behind.
